narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we'd hope—landing at a 60.8% chance of not passing. Picture your story through the model's eyes: it starts with your first-year grades placing you in the bottom decile at school, the single strongest signal that whispered early struggles in handling the intense legal coursework ahead. That pattern held steady by your third year, still in the lowest decile, painting a picture of consistent challenges that weighed heavily on the prediction, like a stubborn shadow over your academic journey. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.8 added to that narrative, suggesting the foundational rigor of law school might stretch you thin, much like it has for others in similar spots. Even your LSAT score of 40 didn't lift the outlook, pulling in the same cautious direction as these academic markers. Your upper-middle family income, while a solid support, surprisingly aligned with profiles that haven't always bridged these gaps in past data. Elements like being a white female pursuing full-time studies rounded out the story, each nudging toward the tougher odds based on patterns we've seen before. It's not that any one piece defined you—it's this blend, like chapters in a book that together forecast hurdles on the bar exam road. But here's the empowering part: this is just a data-driven snapshot, not your destiny, and with targeted steps like bolstering study strategies or extra prep, you could rewrite that ending. You've got resilience in your story already; let's focus on turning these insights into your advantage. We're rooting for you.
